skill.name: web-search
skill.description: Web search and content extraction with Tavily and Exa via inference.sh CLI. Apps: Tavily Search, Tavily Extract, Exa Search, Exa Answer, Exa Extract. Capabilities: AI-powered search, content extraction, direct answers, research. Use for: research, RAG pipelines, fact-checking, content aggregation, agents. Triggers: web search, tavily, exa, search api, content extraction, research, internet search, ai search, search assistant, web scraping, rag, perplexity alternative
skill.summary: Documentation skill that wraps the inference.sh belt CLI to run Tavily and Exa web search and content extraction commands.
skill.author: qu-skills

security_audit.summary: All 39 static findings are false positives. The 25 'Ruby/shell backtick' matches are markdown inline-code formatting, not shell execution. The 10 'Hardcoded URL' matches are legitimate documentation references (inference.sh, example.com). The 4 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' matches have no actual crypto code present. The skill is a 153-line markdown documentation file that declares `allowed-tools: Bash(belt *)` and provides example invocations of the scoped belt CLI for Tavily and Exa search APIs. No obfuscation, no exfiltration patterns, no prompt injection detected.

low_findings.title: Broad shell permission scope
low_findings.description: The skill declares `allowed-tools: Bash(belt *)` which permits any belt CLI subcommand. This is expected for a CLI wrapper skill but is broader than narrowly-scoped alternatives. No malicious behavior observed; the documented subcommands are limited to `belt login`, `belt app run`, and `belt app store`.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Pattern is broad but context shows legitimate CLI wrapper usage with documented and reproducible subcommands only.

content.user_title: Search the Web with Tavily and Exa
content.value_statement: Running multiple web search and content extraction APIs requires learning different CLIs and SDKs. This skill provides ready-made commands for Tavily and Exa through a single belt CLI, letting Claude complete research tasks in one step.
actual_capabilities: Run Tavily search-assistant queries that return AI-generated answers with source citations
Extract clean text and images from multiple URLs using Tavily Extract
Run Exa Search for contextually relevant web links
Get direct factual answers from Exa Answer
Extract and analyze content from individual URLs via Exa Extract
Chain search and LLM steps to build research and RAG pipelines through the belt CLI
limitations: Requires installation and login to the inference.sh belt CLI before use
API usage is subject to Tavily and Exa rate limits and pricing
The skill is documentation-only; it does not implement search logic itself
Output quality depends on the upstream search providers, not the skill

use_cases.title: Topic research for analysts
use_cases.description: Quickly gather sourced information on any topic by running Tavily search-assistant and reviewing cited results.

use_cases.title: RAG pipeline construction
use_cases.description: Combine Tavily or Exa search with an LLM step to build retrieval-augmented generation workflows in Claude.

use_cases.title: Article summarization
use_cases.description: Extract long-form content from URLs with Tavily or Exa Extract, then summarize it with an LLM in a second step.

prompt_templates.title: Basic web search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search the web for the latest developments in quantum computing using Tavily.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user running a simple search

prompt_templates.title: Extract content from a URL
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract the main text from https://example.com/long-article using Tavily Extract and save it to content.json.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to grab an article for offline analysis

prompt_templates.title: Direct factual answer
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Exa Answer to find the current population of Tokyo and cite the source.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs a quick factual lookup with a citation

prompt_templates.title: Search plus LLM analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for the top machine learning frameworks in 2024 with Exa, then ask Claude to compare their strengths and weaknesses.
prompt_templates.scenario: Power user chaining search and LLM for a comparison report

output_examples.input: Search for 'best practices for building AI agents' using Tavily.
output: AI-generated answer summarizing the top recommendations
List of cited source URLs with titles and snippets
Optional related images returned by the search assistant

output_examples.input: Extract content from two article URLs using Tavily Extract.
output: Cleaned text body for each URL
Image references extracted from the pages
Metadata such as title and publication date when available

best_practices: Run `belt login` once before the first invocation to authenticate the CLI
Use a single belt app run command per request rather than chaining inside one shell call
Pipe search output to a file when you plan to feed it into a second LLM step
anti_patterns: Do not paste API keys directly into belt input; use `belt login` for credentials
Do not combine unrelated search queries in one --input payload; run them separately
Do not assume the skill handles authentication for you; verify belt login status first

faq.question: What is the belt CLI?
faq.answer: The belt CLI is the command-line client for inference.sh. It runs apps such as Tavily and Exa search from the terminal.

faq.question: Do I need an API key for Tavily or Exa?
faq.answer: No separate key is required. Authentication is handled by the belt CLI through `belt login`.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: How do I run a Tavily search?
faq.answer: Run `belt app run tavily/search-assistant --input '{"query": "your query"}'` from the terminal.

faq.question: Can I chain search results with an LLM?
faq.answer: Yes. Save the first command output to a file, then run a second belt command that calls an LLM model with that content in the prompt.

faq.question: Does the skill need network access?
faq.answer: Yes. The belt CLI makes outbound requests to Tavily, Exa, and inference.sh to process queries.
